environment.note: This is a CSS feature-support and rendering-behavior check, not a timing benchmark. CPU/GPU/RAM do not affect the result, so they are intentionally omitted.

subject.name: chromium
subject.note: Chromium build bundled with the pinned Playwright version below.

baseline.name: floating-ui
baseline.note: Compared conceptually in the post (what CSS now covers vs what still needs JS). No Floating UI code was executed in this run; the comparison is by capability, not by measured timing.

cross_browser.firefox: NOT measured. Local Playwright browser revision mismatch (package expected firefox-1511; a different revision was installed). Firefox support in the post is cited from caniuse/MDN, explicitly flagged as not-firsthand.
cross_browser.webkit: NOT measured. Same reason (expected webkit-2272). Safari support is cited, not measured.

raw_artifacts.retention_policy: none discarded — all evidence for this run is small and committed
raw_artifacts.note: This run produces no large raw artifacts. render-chromium.png is an illustrative screenshot, regenerable by run.sh; it is intentionally not committed (screenshots are non-deterministic and would not hash-match on re-run). The deterministic evidence — probe-result.json (the CSS.supports() matrix) — is committed.
